mapping.a_element: small nonlinearity (cubic term) as the perturbation off a periodic linear-oscillator base state
mapping.b_element: magnetic vector potential as the perturbation off a periodic Grover-walk base state

mapping.a_element: periodicity of the unperturbed solution one wants to preserve
mapping.b_element: periodicity of the unperturbed Grover walk whose robustness is being tested

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the generic idea of perturbing a system whose base state is periodic; the actual machinery differs. Paper A uses the Lindstedt-Poincare frequency-straining asymptotic expansion to kill secular terms in a single nonlinear ODE, whereas Paper B uses linear degenerate spectral (eigenvalue) perturbation theory of a unitary operator, deriving an effective Hermitian generator from non-simple eigenvalues -- there is no role-for-role counterpart (no frequency-straining/secular-term removal in B, no eigenvalue-degeneracy/effective-Hamiltonian structure in A).
